lines: Norway are absolutely carrying this sweepstake on their backs — Al, Butters, Callum, Croft, and Wilmot all cashing in as they dumped Côte d'Ivoire out 2-1, while the Morocco four-way love triangle (Butters, Callum, Jim, Wilmot) watched their girl draw 1-1 with the Dutch. Penalties are a thing, lads.
Butters sits top on 59pts with Portugal, Morocco, Norway AND DR Congo still breathing — at this point he's not entering a sweepstake, he's running a fucking cartel.

Carter's quietly lurking in 2nd on 49pts with England, Colombia, Algeria and DR Congo all still in — four teams, all alive, saying absolutely nothing and just getting on with it like a serial killer in a Greggs queue.

Today's big one: France vs Sweden means Callum, Croft, Dene and The Foreman are all backing Les Bleus, while Jim — sitting 6th on 38pts — is praying his Sweden somehow pull off an upset. Jim, mate, Sweden have looked like a team assembled from a FIFA career mode budget save, so temper your expectations.

Special mention to Ellis, who watched South Korea and New Zealand get binned in the group stage while Paraguay scraped a 1-1 with Germany — three teams in, two already dead, one barely alive on life support. Get fucked, Ellis, that draw is doing nothing for you sitting 8th on 33pts.

Eyes on France vs Sweden today — if France win comfortably, four of the top ten sweepstake holders get a boost, but Jim's Sweden need to pull something special out of their arses or he stays marooned mid-table. Morocco watching from the bench after yesterday's draw too — penalties incoming, lads, stock up.
